serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Asparagus Stir Fried with Scallops is approximately one plate, weighing around 250 grams.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central, SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values estimated based on a composite of cooked scallops (ID: 151591), cooked asparagus (ID: 11009), soybean oil (ID: 04044), and light soy sauce (ID: 16124) from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Extended micronutrient breakdown was not calculable from the composite estimation and is reported as -1 per sentinel value convention.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index estimated based on the low carbohydrate content of scallops and asparagus, and typical GI values for non-starchy vegetable and seafood stir-fries. No direct entry found for this specific dish.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume this dish in moderation due to its high sodium content (~424 mg per 100 g), and consider requesting reduced-sodium sauces when ordering.

type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low carbohydrate (3.2 g per 100 g) and sugar content (0.9 g per 100 g), contributing to stable postprandial blood glucose levels.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat and saturated fat from cooking oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While scallops are a lean protein source, the total fat content (~15.4 g per 100 g) and saturated fat (~2.4 g per 100 g) from cooking oil are moderate; individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume in moderation and opt for preparations with less added oil.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Asparagus Stir Fried with Scallops is a popular Malaysian-style Zi Char dish featuring tender asparagus spears and succulent scallops quickly cooked in a hot wok. Typically, it involves stir-frying garlic, then adding the scallops and asparagus, often with a savory sauce made from soy sauce, oyster sauce, and a touch of sugar. The dish offers a delightful contrast in textures, with the crisp-tender asparagus complementing the soft, slightly chewy scallops. It is known for its fresh, umami-rich flavor profile. Nutritionally, it is a good source of protein from the scallops and provides dietary fiber and vitamins from the asparagus. Due to its low carbohydrate content, it has a low glycemic index, making it a suitable option for managing blood sugar. However, the sodium content can be high depending on the amount of sauce used, which is a consideration for individuals with hypertension. The fat content varies with the amount of cooking oil used during preparation.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values and glycemic index are estimated from component ingredients from approved sources (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, University of Sydney GI Database), as no direct entry for this specific dish was found in any primary regional or global approved source.
